players in the online e-commerce space, such as Amazon,
Netﬂix and Alibaba, the product recommendation function-
ality is now a key driver of demand, accounting in the case
of Amazon for roughly 35% [2] of the overall sales.

mendation performance on a subset of the 30Music listening
and playlists dataset [30] with a low implementation and
integration cost.

Meta-Prod2Vec
As shown in the Related Work section, there has been ex-
tensive work on using side information for recommendation,
especially in the context of hybrid methods that combine
CF methods and Content-based (CB) methods.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

Improvements on Cold-Start
Most of the gains in performance are coming from the

large margin all other methods when the true pair of (query

concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
CONCLUSIONS
In this paper, we introduced Meta-Prod2Vec, a new item
embedding method that enhances the existing Prod2Vec method
with item metadata at training time.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
This work makes a
novel connection between the recent embedding-based meth-
ods and consecrated Matrix Factorization methods by in-
troducing learning with side information in the context of
embeddings.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Finally, we have shown that
Meta-Prod2Vec constantly outperforms Prod2Vec on recom-
mendation tasks both globally and in the cold-start regime,
and that, when combined with a standard Collaborative
Filtering approach, outperforms all other tested methods.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
These results, together with the reduced implementation
cost and the fact that our method does not aﬀect the online
recommendation architecture, makes this solution attractive
in cases where item embeddings are already in use.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Such scenarios are frequently encountered in
applications such as content recommendation, ad targeting
and web search.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Our method leverages past user interactions
with items and their attributes to compute low-dimensional
embeddings of items.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
We show that the new item representa-
tions lead to better performance on recommendation tasks
on an open music dataset.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
